text.title: SLO, SLI, and Error Budget
text.summary: Defines the measurement vocabulary (SLI), targets (SLO), and engineering budget (error budget) that translate business uptime goals into actionable constraints.

rule.statement: Choose SLIs that measure user-visible behaviour, not internal component health.
rule.rationale: Internal CPU metrics do not tell you whether users are experiencing failures. Measuring from the user perspective (successful requests / total requests) ensures the SLO reflects real service quality.

rule.statement: Set SLO targets slightly stricter than SLA to preserve an internal buffer before contractual breach.
rule.rationale: If SLA = 99.9%, set SLO = 99.95%. The gap is the operational buffer for undetected degradation.

section.title: SLI Types by Service
section.p: Common SLIs mapped to service categories:

list.item: API: request latency (p50/p95/p99), error rate (5xx / total), throughput
Database: query latency, availability, replication lag
Storage: durability (data loss probability), read/write latency
Streaming: end-to-end latency, message loss rate, consumer lag
Batch: success rate, processing duration, data freshness

section.title: Availability Tiers
section.p: Map business context to availability target before choosing patterns:

list.item: 99% (2 nines): 3.65 days/year downtime — internal tools, dev environments
99.9% (3 nines): 8.76 hours/year — business applications, growth-stage products
99.99% (4 nines): 52.6 minutes/year — critical services, e-commerce checkout

rule.statement: Calculate monthly error budget as (1 - SLO) * 43,200 minutes and enforce a feature freeze when it is exhausted.
rule.rationale: Error budget converts an abstract percentage into a concrete time allowance that engineering and product can negotiate. Exhausted budget means reliability work takes priority over features.

example.note: SLO = 99.95% → error budget = 0.05% * 43,200 min = 21.6 min/month. Alert at 50% consumed (10.8 min used) to trigger reliability review before breach.

rule.statement: Use rolling 30-day windows for error budget calculation, not calendar months.
rule.rationale: Rolling windows avoid cliff effects where budget resets at month boundary, hiding sustained slow degradation.

text.reference: SLO document template with SLI expression, target, window, and escalation thresholds
